design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate large 2D platforming levels with multi-step objectives (passing through hoops, platform sequences, riding roller coasters) to unlock exit warps; collect points and stars to power attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Instant-death spike placement in awkwardly positioned locations creates moment-to-moment navigation tension; limited star ammo for ranged attacks creates resource management decisions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Aero, the circus' greatest acrobatic star, performing aerial tricks and drill attacks to rescue kidnapped performers and stop an evil industrialist's sabotage
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game commits to punishing level design with instant-death spikes and large, complex level layouts requiring careful memorization. Rejects traditional linear progression in favor of objective-based level completion. Bonus levels tied to point thresholds reward high-score play.

difficulty.lives_system: Lives system exists; hidden cheat codes allow infinite lives adjustment
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpointing documented; level-based checkpoint structure only
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select codes and cheat options (infinite lives, infinite stars, no enemy collisions) provide advanced players with difficulty modulation tools; suggests developer expected mastery-oriented audience

progression.upgrade_system: Star ammo collection for ranged attacks; point accumulation unlocks bonus levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Multi-step objectives within each level (hoop passes, platform sequences, roller coaster rides) must be completed to reveal exit warp; bonus levels unlocked by meeting point thresholds at level end
replayability_hooks: Bonus level access tied to performance thresholds
Level select and cheat codes enable speedrun/challenge play
Four worlds with five levels each provide structured replay loop

world_building_techniques: Character motivation setup: Edgar Ektor's backstory (banned from circus, became evil industrialist) establishes villain's motive
Relationship grounding: Aero must rescue girlfriend Aeriel among other performers
Thematic setting: Circus/funpark environment establishes acrobatic performance as narrative context
Gang structure: Psycho Circus gang adds antagonistic force flavor

faction_indicators: Aero: circus performer, heroic acrobat, rescue protagonist
Edgar Ektor: wealthy industrialist villain, saboteur
Psycho Circus gang: antagonistic collective serving Edgar
Circus performers: kidnapped innocents requiring rescue
danger_signals: Large, complex level layouts with high memorization demands
Aerial drill attack requires precise positioning
environmental_storytelling: Circus/funpark setting establishes performance and entertainment theme
Sabotaged funpark state indicates villain's destructive impact
Roller coaster ride sequences reinforce carnival amusement park aesthetic
Pool diving bonus level sequences connect to circus acrobatic tradition

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Aero the Acro-Bat (SNES)
